home.openExplanation: Click here for a brief explanation!
home.intro1: Hello! Welcome to Glimpse, a platform where you learn the basics of coding while creating visuals.
home.intro2: There are two pages, and you’re free to start wherever you like.
home.oefeningenExplanation: On the exercises page, you’ll find a short introduction to help you get started with coding, followed by a few key concepts.
home.createExplanation: If you prefer to start creating visuals right away, go to the create page.
home.step1: Choose an example from the list.
home.step2: Click ▶ next to a parameter to view the corresponding code.
home.step3: Want to see everything? Click "Show all code" at the bottom.

login.resetEmailSent: Check your email for the reset link.

basis.title: Let's start!
basis.buildingBlocks: A website consists of three building blocks each with their own task:
basis.htmlDesc:
This is the skeleton of the page. It determines what appears on the page: headings, text, images, buttons, links. Everything is in "tags" like `

Title

` or `

A paragraph

`.
basis.cssDesc: This is the styling. It determines how the HTML looks: colors, fonts, spacing, layout. Without CSS, a website looks bare and boring.
basis.jsDesc: This makes the page interactive. Think of a menu that opens, a button that responds, or data that loads without refreshing the page.
basis.technicalIntro: When you type a website address in your browser, this happens:
basis.step1: Your browser sends a request to a server (a computer somewhere on the internet)
basis.step2: The server sends files back, the HTML, CSS and JavaScript
basis.step3: Your browser builds the page based on those files
basis.frontendDesc: HTML, CSS, JS: what you see in the browser
basis.backendDesc: The server, database, logic behind the scenes
basis.simpleVsComplex: A simple website (like a business card) only has a frontend. A more complex website (like a webshop) also has a backend to store orders and users, for example.
basis.summary: In short: HTML = structure, CSS = appearance, JavaScript = behavior, and a server delivers those files to your browser.

oefening1.title: Basic HTML
oefening1.htmlDesc: HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the language for building web pages.
oefening1.html: Main element of every HTML page
oefening1.head: Contains meta-information (title, styles, scripts)
oefening1.exercise1Desc: Create a basic HTML page with:
oefening1.valH1Text: Add the text 'Hello World' to the h1
oefening1.valSuccess1: Well done! Your basic HTML structure is complete.
oefening1.exercise2Req1:
A

element with text "This is my first web page."
oefening1.exercise2Req2: A <span> element inside the paragraph with "awesome"
oefening1.valPText: Add the text 'This is my first web page.'
oefening1.valSpanText: Add the word 'awesome' to the span
oefening1.valSuccess2: Well done! You have correctly used all HTML elements.

oefening2.title: Classes and IDs in CSS
oefening2.classIdDesc: Classes and IDs are ways to identify and style HTML elements.
oefening2.classDesc: Class (.name): Can be used multiple times on the same page. Use a period (.) before the class name.
oefening2.idDesc: ID (#name): Must be unique on a page. Use a hashtag (#) before the ID.
oefening2.exercise1Desc2: Write CSS to style the class "button" with a red background.
oefening2.placeholder: Write your CSS for class "button" here
oefening2.valSuccess1: Well done! You have correctly styled the class.
oefening2.exercise2Desc2: Write CSS to style the ID "masthead" with a font size of 32px.
oefening2.placeholder2: Write your CSS for ID "masthead" here
oefening2.valSuccess2: Well done! You have correctly styled the ID.

oefening3.title: If Statement
oefening3.introTitle: For the following exercises, we will focus on back-end concepts. To get started, here is a brief explanation of the key concepts you need to know.
oefening3.backendThreeThings: The back-end roughly consists of three things:
oefening3.serverDesc: The server : this is a computer that is always on and waiting for requests. When you click a button, the browser sends a message to the server: "give me that data" or "save this". The server processes that request and sends a response back.
oefening3.logicDesc: The application logic : this is the code that determines exactly what needs to happen. Is this user allowed to log in? What is the price after discount? Which messages can he see? All those rules are here.
oefening3.databaseDesc: The database : this is where all data is stored. Usernames, passwords, orders, messages... everything the website needs to remember is stored here.
oefening3.flowDesc: The flow is simple: you click on something → the browser sends a request to the server → the server executes logic and consults the database if necessary → the server sends a response back → the browser displays the result.
oefening3.consoleLogDesc: console.log() is a way to print something in the console (you open it like this: on Windows press F12 or Ctrl + Shift + I and for Mac press Cmd + Option + I), so that as a developer you can see what is happening in your code.
oefening3.ifStatementDesc: An if statement is used to make decisions in your code based on a condition.
oefening3.elseKeyword: what to do if the condition is not true
oefening3.assignment: Write an if statement that checks if someone is an adult.
oefening3.varDefined: The variable age is already defined
oefening3.valUseIf: Use an if statement with the >= operator.
oefening3.valSuccess: Well done! Your if statement works correctly!

oefening4.title: Arrays
oefening4.arrayDesc: An array is a list of values that you can store and access via an index.
oefening4.index: Index: Arrays start at 0, so the first element is 0.
oefening4.exercise1Desc: Create an array numbers with the values [5, 10, 15]. Print the first element (index 0).
oefening4.valBrackets: Create an array with square brackets
oefening4.valValues: Make sure the array contains the values 5, 10 and 15
oefening4.valIndex0: Use index [0] to access the first element
oefening4.valConsoleLog: Use console.log to print the element
oefening4.valSuccess1: Well done! You have correctly created and accessed the array.
oefening4.exercise2Desc2: Use push() to add "Chris". Use console.log(names) to print the array.
oefening4.valSuccess2: Well done! You have correctly used push().

oefening5.title: Boolean: true / false
oefening5.booleanDesc: A boolean is a data type that can have two values: true or false.
oefening5.exercise1Desc: Create a variable isActive with the value true. Create a variable isDisabled with the value false.
oefening5.valSuccess1: Well done! You have correctly created both boolean variables.
oefening5.exercise2Desc: Check if 15 is greater than 10 and print the result. Use a comparison with >.
oefening5.valSuccess2: Well done! You have correctly executed the comparison.

oefening6.title: Data Structures
oefening6.dataStructuresDesc: In JavaScript, there are different ways to organize data.
oefening6.arrayDesc: An ordered list of things, like a shopping list.
oefening6.objectDesc: Information about one thing, like an ID card with a name, age and address.
oefening6.mapDesc: Similar to an object but more flexible — you can use anything as a label.
oefening6.setDesc: A list like an array, but each item can only appear once.
oefening6.exercise1Desc: Create an object car with: - brand: "Toyota" - year: 2020 Print the brand with console.log.
oefening6.valCurlyBraces: Create an object with curly braces
oefening6.valSuccess1: Well done! You have correctly created the object.
oefening6.exercise2Desc: Create an array students with two objects: - first student: name "Anna", grade 8 - second student: name "Bob", grade 7 Print the entire array.
oefening6.valSquareBrackets: Create an array with square brackets
oefening6.valSuccess2: Well done! You have correctly created the nested data structure.

oefening7.title: Variables: let, var and const
oefening7.variablesDesc: In coding, there are three ways to declare variables: let, var and const.
oefening7.letDesc: You can change the value later, but you cannot create the same variable twice in the same place.
oefening7.varDesc: This is an older version of 'let'. It allows everything: changing the value and recreating the variable.
oefening7.constDesc: The value is fixed. Once set, it cannot be changed.
oefening7.exercise1Desc: Declare a variable with let named temperature with the value 20. Then assign a new value 25 to the same variable.
oefening7.valUseLet: Use let to declare the variable
oefening7.valSuccess1: Well done! You have correctly used let with reassignment.
oefening7.exercise2Desc: Declare a constant variable with const named maxScore with the value 100. Print the constant with console.log.
oefening7.valUseConst: Use const to declare the variable
oefening7.valSuccess2: Well done! You have correctly used const.

oefening8.title: For Loops
oefening8.forLoopDesc: A for loop is used to execute a code block multiple times.
oefening8.exercise1Desc: Write a for loop that prints the numbers 0 through 4 (console.log).
oefening8.valDeclareVar: Declare a variable in the for loop
oefening8.valSuccess1: Well done! Your for loop works correctly.
oefening8.exercise2Desc2: Write a for...of loop that prints each value.
oefening8.valSuccess2: Well done! Your for...of loop works correctly.

resetPassword.title: Set a new password
resetPassword.tooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters.
resetPassword.success: Password changed! You will be redirected...
